fundamentals.name: PayPal Holdings, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: PayPal Holdings, Inc. operates a technology platform that enables digital payments for merchants and consumers worldwide. The company operates a two-sided network at scale that connects merchants and consumers that enables its customers to connect, transact, and send and receive payments through online and in person, as well as transfer and withdraw funds using various funding sources, such as bank accounts, PayPal or Venmo account balance, consumer credit and debit products, credit and debit cards, and cryptocurrencies, as well as other stored value products, including gift cards and eligible rewards. It provides payment solutions under the PayPal, PayPal Credit, Braintree, Venmo, Xoom, Hyperwallet, Honey, and Paidy names. The company was founded in 1998 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

product.name: Revenues From Other Value Added Services

judgment.summary: Profitable with strong cash flow and a low valuation, but facing declining earnings growth and analyst concerns.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates with EPS of $1.34 vs $1.27; next earnings date is 2026-07-28.
positives: Trailing P/E of 8.31 and forward P/E of 7.67 indicate a low valuation.
Free cash flow of $4.08B TTM supports operations and shareholder returns.
Return on equity at 25.1% demonstrates efficient use of shareholder capital.
Gross margin of 40.9% and operating margin of 18.0% show healthy profitability.
Dividend yield of 1.3% offers a modest income stream.
negatives: Revenue growth of 7.2% YoY is modest, while earnings growth is negative (-6.2% YoY).
Quarterly earnings growth has declined by 13.5% YoY.
Analyst estimates project further declines in earnings for the current quarter and fiscal year.
Debt/Equity ratio of 58.28% and total debt exceeding total cash suggest financial leverage.
Quick ratio of 0.22 indicates potential short-term liquidity challenges.

news.paragraph: Recent reports highlight PayPal's (PYPL) Q1 performance being impacted by a leadership change, even as the company navigates the evolving digital payments landscape with AI and stablecoins. While the company faces headwinds, some analysts suggest it is a cash-flow pioneer. The stock trades at a low multiple, but faces scrutiny on growth and execution.

examples.body: $PYPL there is hardly any growth and competition increasing every single day. There is reason its cheap because no one believes in its growth
